name: Montalbán Castle Loop from Los Navalucillos
name_original: Castillo de Montalbán – circular desde Los Navalucillos por Villarejo de Montalbán
description: The Castillo de Montalbán circular mountain biking route is a difficult 48.3-mile ride with 3753 feet of climbing in Montes de Toledo.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Los Navalucillos offers diverse terrain within the Montes de Toledo mountain range, serving as a gateway to the Cabañeros National Park. The region features a mix of mountains and plains, with ancient forests of holm oaks and cork trees, alongside areas of Jara and heather. Riders can explore routes that traverse varied landscapes, including riverbanks and elevated viewpoints. This environment provides a range of challenges and scenic routes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Los Navalucillos The most popular mountain bike trail is Rocigalgo Peak Loop, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends towards Rocigalgo Peak, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Parque Nacional de Cabañeros – circular por Las Becerras y Sierra Fría, a difficult 18.5 miles (29.7 km) path. This route explores the Cabañeros National Park, passing through natural preserves and offering views from Las Becerras. Local mountain bikers also love the La Jara and Pusa River Loop, a 31.7 miles (50.9 km) trail leading through areas of Jara and along the River Pusa, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Los Navalucillos is defined by the Montes de Toledo, the Cabañeros National Park, and the River Pusa. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Los Navalucillos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 6 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Los Navalucillos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Los Navalucillos?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails available around Los Navalucillos, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored 29 tours in total.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Los Navalucillos?
A: Mountain biking around Los Navalucillos offers diverse terrain within the Montes de Toledo mountain range. You'll encounter a mix of mountains and plains, with ancient forests of holm oaks and cork trees, alongside areas of Jara and heather. Routes often traverse varied landscapes, including riverbanks and elevated viewpoints, providing both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Los Navalucillos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Los Navalucillos cater to a range of abilities. Out of 29 tours, 3 are considered easy, 16 are moderate, and 10 are difficult. This means you can find routes suitable for a relaxed ride or a more challenging adventure.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in Los Navalucillos?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls in the area. The notable 'La Chorrera' waterfall, a 15-meter cascade in El Chorro on Monte Rocigalgo, is a prominent natural attraction. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights in the region.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike routes in Los Navalucillos?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. Several viewpoints, or 'miradores,' such as Cerro de las Colmenillas, Risco de Martín Domínguez, Las Becerras, and La Joyona, offer panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026058323/parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-circular-por-las-becerras-y-sierra-fria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Cabañeros – circular por Las Becerras y Sierra Fría&lt;/a&gt; specifically pass through areas with great views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Los Navalucillos?
A: While many routes in Los Navalucillos are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy trails available that could be suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain, allowing for a more enjoyable experience with children.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass through the Cabañeros National Park?
A: Yes, Los Navalucillos serves as a gateway to the Cabañeros National Park, which is known for its well-preserved Mediterranean forest. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026058323/parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-circular-por-las-becerras-y-sierra-fria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Cabañeros – circular por Las Becerras y Sierra Fría&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through this significant natural preserve, offering a chance to experience its rich biodiversity.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Los Navalucillos?
A: The Montes de Toledo region, where Los Navalucillos is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures, though riding is still possible on many days.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Los Navalucillos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023042192/rocigalgo-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocigalgo Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026339960/la-jara-and-pusa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Jara and Pusa River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the River Pusa.

Q: Can I ride along the River Pusa on a mountain bike?
A: Yes, you can. The River Pusa offers tranquil natural settings, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026339960/la-jara-and-pusa-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Jara and Pusa River Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically lead along its banks, allowing you to enjoy the riverside scenery while mountain biking.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Los Navalucillos?
A: The mountain bike routes in Los Navalucillos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 6 reviews. Over 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the immersive natural experience within the Montes de Toledo and near Cabañeros National Park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting natural monuments to see while mountain biking?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026357377/montalban-castle-loop-from-los-navalucillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montalbán Castle Loop from Los Navalucillos&lt;/a&gt; offers a historical point of interest. Additionally, natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5636470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant Marine Worm Tracks&lt;/a&gt; are highlights in the area that you might pass or visit.
tips: Originally built as a Muslim citadel and later taken over by the Templars, it is so robust that there is no evidence that it was ever attacked. A visit is an opportunity to experience a defensive castle, in its most literal sense.

Somewhat hidden but not forgotten, the Roman bridge of Villarejo de Montalbán is another clear example of the Roman occupation of these lands. Built in the 3rd century to save the course of the Cedena River, it currently still retains its structure and, even more importantly, its function.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route, covering approximately 77.7 kilometers, is estimated to take around 6 hours and 39 minutes to complete. This duration assumes a good pace from experienced riders with very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its length, significant elevation changes (around 1,140 meters up and 1,150 meters down), and potentially technical terrain. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where is the main trailhead and parking for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Los Navalucillos. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors accessing trails in the region.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Castillo de Montalbán mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Expect a diverse and demanding terrain. The route likely includes a mix of dirt tracks, potentially loose gravel, rocky sections, and technical descents. The landscape features solitary areas with predominant holm oaks, thyme, and kermes oaks, offering a rustic and natural backdrop.

faqs.question: What notable historical sites or natural features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of historical and natural scenery. You'll pass by the impressive Montalbán Castle, a robust defensive structure with a rich history, and the Roman Bridge of Villarejo. The trail also provides views of the Torcón river and landscapes characterized by holm oaks, thyme, and kermes oaks.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the long and demanding ride more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot in this region of Spain, and winters can bring colder, potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, especially on mountain biking routes. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or in protected natural spaces to ensure their safety and respect for wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Montes de Toledo area?
faqs.answer: For this specific mountain bike route, which traverses public lands, no special permits or entrance fees are typically required. The trail is located within the Montes de Toledo region, but general access for activities like mountain biking does not usually incur fees. Always check local regulations if you plan to deviate from established paths.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino de los Montes de Toledo - Etapa 4, Sendero Calancho, Vereda Cabritera, Camino de las Piedras, and Camino de Valdetravieso, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Los Navalucillos, where you can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Villarejo de Montalbán, another point along the circular route, may also offer limited facilities. Given the trail's length and demanding nature, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as services might be sparse between towns.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a challenging mountain bike ride like this?
faqs.answer: For a ride of this difficulty and length, essential items include plenty of water, energy-rich snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, navigation tools (GPS device or komoot app with offline maps), appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Given the remote sections, consider packing extra supplies.
